channel.title: The Weekend Intelligence
channel.description: The Economist — The Intelligence — The Weekend Intelligence

item.description: Just one generation after they gained independence, people in the Baltic States are watching the threat from the Kremlin creep closer and closer. In…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Inside Nepal’s Gen Z Revolution
item.description: Nepal’s youth took to the streets last September when the government banned social media. It became a protest about corruption. Police killed 19…

item.description: When Yamagami Tetsuya fired the bullets that killed Japan’s longest-serving prime minister, he set off a chain reaction that upended Japanese…

item.description: In the outer suburbs of Istanbul, the Elithair clinic operates nearly round the clock delivering hair transplants to a seemingly endless stream of…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Honouring the dead
item.description: While walking around a national cemetery, it can seem like memorialising the war dead is a given, but governments have not always honoured their…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Sweating It Out
item.description: Britain is getting hot and steamy. From railway arches in Peckham to windswept beaches in Fife, public saunas are popping up across the country,…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Re-run: The last boat
item.description: We asked the head of podcasts to choose his favourite Weekend Intelligence from 2025 to play during the Christmas period. He chose our host Rosie…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: 2025, a year in the making
item.description: This week we’re taking a different approach, returning to some of our most compelling episodes from the past year. We’ll discover what’s happened to…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Return to Iran
item.description: In July 2019 Nicolas Pelham, The Economist’s Middle East correspondent, received a rare journalist’s visa to Iran. On the day he was due to fly…

item.description: Ukraine has been hit by a corruption scandal. One that strikes at the core of the political establishment in a way never before seen—and this in a…

item.description: Chef Gary Thomas has a lot on his plate. That’s because he’s in the business of feeding thousands of people a day on a ship in the middle of the…

item.description: Seventy years ago, a small Christian community in rural Georgia became an unlikely battleground in America’s fight over civil rights. Koinonia was…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Welcome to Culiacán
item.description: Since September 2024 the Sinaloa Cartel has been tearing itself apart, and the city of Culiacán has been dragged into its bloody and brutal…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: The return of Erik Prince
item.description: Erik Prince, America’s most infamous mercenary, is back.  Mr Prince rose to prominence during the war on terror as the founder of Blackwater, a…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Science under siege
item.description: It's been a tough year for American universities.  The Trump administration has taken aim at elite institutions across the country, threatening…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Anatomy of an orchestra
item.description: In orchestras, everyone has a place. Third trumpet, second violin, concertmaster. Your place determines not just where you sit, but your social…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: North Korea’s dark turn
item.description: Trying to observe life in North Korea is like looking through a glass, darkly. Since journalists can’t go in, and hardly anyone comes out, the only…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Cold War Island
item.description: Adak, a windswept island off the coast of Alaska, was transformed into a military stronghold during the second world war. During the cold war it…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: We don’t need no (AI) education
item.description: In lecture halls and seminar rooms across America, a quiet revolution is underway. Students are using AI to write essays, solve problems, and even…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Farage’s power project
item.description: At their Birmingham conference last week, Reform UK, once dismissed as a fringe movement, had the hallmarks of a party preparing for government.…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Studying in the shadows
item.description: On August 15th 2021 the Taliban walked into Kabul and seized control of the capital without firing a shot. Time stood still, as many Afghans were…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: 20 years on from Katrina
item.description: 20 years on from Hurricane Katrina and New Orleans has some of the most impressive flood defences in the world. The city was rebuilt at enormous…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: The last boat
item.description: The Economist's Rosie Blau grew up knowing that her father escaped the Nazi regime on the last boat from Poland to Britain before war broke out in…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: Inside Opus Dei
item.description: Growing up in Barcelona, Carla Subirana was surrounded by schools and universities linked to Opus Dei. The organisation, part of the Catholic…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: The Memory Keepers
item.description: In Japan, the last witnesses to the horror of nuclear destruction are approaching the end of their lives. And as they die out, nuclear weapons no…

item.title: The Weekend Intelligence: The world’s toughest exam
item.description: Thirty million Indians want a job on the railways, but a fiendish general-knowledge test stands in their way. Could you “Explain America's policy on…
